title: Third-Party Actions, Pinning, Dependency Trust, and Workflow CodeQL · Independent Delivery Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer third-party actions, pinning, dependency trust, and workflow codeql into a new action dependency and update policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of action dependency and update policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance.
Pin third-party actions to reviewed full commit SHAs, retain readable version comments, and automate reviewed update proposals.
Inventory direct, reusable, composite, Docker, and nested action dependencies plus their effective privilege and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review, keep Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-actions-codeql-policy-enforcement-x0jg9b". Tailor its action-dependency-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, keep Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-actions-codeql-policy-enforcement-x0jg9b
  - competency: ci-actions-codeql-policy-enforcement
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: actions codeql policy enforcement
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: change an action tag to a different commit without workflow edits
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: x0jg9b
    change: |
      allowed-actions: reviewed-publishers
      third-party-pin: full-commit-sha
      version-comment: retained
      nested-inventory: generated
      effective-permissions: reviewed
      workflow-codeowners: delivery-security
      update: automated-pr-with-compat-tests
      analysis: codeql-actions-and-policy
      revocation: rehearsed
    verify: enumerate nested actions resolve immutable SHAs inspect bundled runtime inputs post steps permissions advisories and update evidence
    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
    repair: Review effective code and authority, pin immutable revisions, protect workflow changes, test updates, enforce policy, and rehearse removal.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, keep Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Static analysis proves a workflow is safe under every event payload and runner condition.
